customer_ready_scope: module-local visible-output equivalence for C910 ct_rtu_pst_preg_entry under reset-first via passive debug bridge plus closed lifecycle-encoding relation induction; legacy proof packet only; full-PPA authority lives in the separate same-candidate metric packet

candidate.summary: Recode the lifecycle FSM from 5-bit one-hot to 4-bit onehot0 with WF_ALLOC represented as zero, preserving visible RTU PST entry outputs under the lifecycle encoding relation.
candidate.state_change: lifecycle FSM state re-encoded; same-state bit equivalence is intentionally not claimed

exact_output_bmc_seq12.reset_contract: cpurst_b=0 at step 1, cpurst_b=1 at step 2; clocks/enables held active, scan held low

no_reset_boundary_bmc_seq6.interpretation: Dropping the reset-first contract makes the exact-output miter fail, so the reset boundary is material.

raw_exact_output_tempinduct_seq8.interpretation: Raw exact-output induction proves bounded base cases deeply but does not close the induction step. Do not use this log as unbounded authority.

same_state_equivalence_boundary.interpretation: Same-state bit equivalence leaves lifecycle/retire-derived cells unproven because the candidate intentionally changes the lifecycle state encoding.

lifecycle_encoding_relation_tempinduct_s.interpretation: Temporal induction closes over passive debug copies asserting lifecycle encoding relation, storage/retire state relation, and visible-output equality.

relation_mutant_negative.interpretation: The relation miter rejects a broken visible-output candidate, so the relation proof shape is discriminating.

debug_wrapper_passivity.interpretation: bridge_passivity_check.py normalizes debug wrappers back to exact RTL by stripping only dbg_* ports, declarations, and assignments. Its selftest rejects a non-passive logic edit.

known_boundaries: scoped evidence only (not current-bar customer-ready) for scoped module-local visible-output equivalence under reset-first via passive debug bridge plus lifecycle-encoding relation induction.
The exact output miter is bounded to seq12; the unbounded positive path is the passive-debug lifecycle relation proof.
Same-state bit equivalence is explicitly not claimed because lifecycle state encoding changes; the 304 proven / 6 unproven split is retained only as a boundary artifact.
Raw exact-output temporal induction non-closure remains a boundary artifact, not the positive authority path.
Legacy proof packet: selected Sky130 local and top-with-deps area improve here; full-PPA claims require the separate same-candidate metric packet.
No whole RTU, whole C910, BOOM, ISA, memory-consistency, speculation, composed-optimization, or whole-chip authority is claimed.
review_ruling: Ronald cold replay and Quan adversarial QA support promotion to accepted module-local visible-output relation packet with the stated boundaries.
